articles.title: Important Dates and What They Mean
articles.sourceFile: C:\Users\Myburgh Swiegers\Projects\Codex_GDI\content\education\raw\Important Dates and What They Mean.docx

blocks.text: “First Notice Day” – What does that mean and what does it represent:Fist Notice Day is the first day of the new trading month which long position holders could receive delivery on their futures via the random allocation process.
If a trader wishes to receive physical stock they do not need to do anything and will receive their physical stock at the randomly selected silo.

However if the trader does not wish to receive delivery they need to close their long position(s) either buy selling a future contract or buying the spread (which in turn will close the long future). If the trader selected to Buy the spread their long would have been rolled on to a different month.
